title: Unjust Murder (Killing an Innocent Soul)
subtitle: The Most Severe Crime Against Humanity in the Quran

verses.arabic: مَن قَتَلَ نَفْسًا بِغَيْرِ نَفْسٍ أَوْ فَسَادٍ فِي الْأَرْضِ فَكَأَنَّمَا قَتَلَ النَّاسَ جَمِيعًا وَمَنْ أَحْيَاهَا فَكَأَنَّمَا أَحْيَا النَّاسَ جَمِيعًا
verses.translation: Whoever kills a soul unless for a soul or for corruption [done] in the land — it is as if he had slain mankind entirely. And whoever saves one — it is as if he had saved mankind entirely.

verses.arabic: وَمَن يَقْتُلْ مُؤْمِنًا مُّتَعَمِّدًا فَجَزَاؤُهُ جَهَنَّمُ خَالِدًا فِيهَا وَغَضِبَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَلَعَنَهُ وَأَعَدَّ لَهُ عَذَابًا عَظِيمًا
verses.translation: But whoever kills a believer intentionally — his recompense is Hell, wherein he will abide eternally, and Allah has become angry with him and has cursed him and has prepared for him a great punishment.

verses.arabic: وَلَا تَقْتُلُوا النَّفْسَ الَّتِي حَرَّمَ اللَّهُ إِلَّا بِالْحَقِّ
verses.translation: And do not kill the soul which Allah has forbidden [to be killed] except by [legal] right.

verses.arabic: وَالَّذِينَ لَا يَدْعُونَ مَعَ اللَّهِ إِلَٰهًا آخَرَ وَلَا يَقْتُلُونَ النَّفْسَ الَّتِي حَرَّمَ اللَّهُ إِلَّا بِالْحَقِّ وَلَا يَزْنُونَ
verses.translation: And those who do not invoke with Allah another deity or kill the soul which Allah has forbidden [to be killed], except by right, and do not commit unlawful sexual intercourse.

brief_explanation: Allah has declared human life sacred, and the Quran equates the unjust killing of one soul to the killing of all of humanity — a scale of evil unmatched among worldly crimes. The sin of intentional murder brings upon the killer Allah's anger, curse, and eternal punishment in Hell, as explicitly stated in Surah An-Nisa (4:93). The Quran forbids the killing of any soul 'which Allah has made sacred' except through just legal process, establishing that this right belongs solely to sanctioned authority, not to individuals. No worldly benefit or personal grievance justifies taking a life unjustly in the sight of Allah.
capital_punishment: Intentional murder (qatl al-amd) carries the hadd of qisas — the right of the victim's family to demand execution of the murderer. This is established in the Quran (2:178) and agreed upon by all four major madhabs (Hanafi, Maliki, Shafi'i, Hanbali). The family of the victim may also accept blood money (diyah) or pardon the killer entirely, which is regarded as an act of virtue. The state may also pursue ta'zir (discretionary punishment) if qisas is waived.
